presentation_options.text: What is different in meaning between “le” and “le”? What does the extra part do?
Starting from “le” (meaning ‘le’), how do you say it when it is a different person, number, or tense?
If we change the subject from “le” to “le”, does “le” change? How?
Inside “le”, is “le” a piece you can take off and still have a word, or does it only ever appear stuck on?
Is there a difference between “le” and “le”? Do they mean different things, or are they the same word said two ways?
Can you say “le”? Does it sound right / natural to you?
Which of these sounds most natural: ‘le’? Are any of them wrong?
